vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-711: PAN-705 follow-up: remove transitional route aliases after merge

plan.title: PAN-705 follow-up: remove transitional route aliases after merge
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: PAN-705 originally landed with backward-compat alias routes that mirrored the old /api/workspaces/:id/{review-status,approve,merge,...} paths to the renamed /api/review/* and /api/issues/* handlers. The aliases were intentional tech debt to let specialists run on the PAN-705 branch against a stale main-built dashboard. PAN-711 was filed to delete them once main was rebuilt. During PAN-705 review (feedback round #007), the review agent flagged the aliases as a 'no bandaids' violation and they were deleted in commit d18c74cc, which was squashed into the merged PR #708. Source on main is already alias-free. What remains is (a) preventing them from being reintroduced and (b) sweeping a handful of living docs that still cite the old paths as authoritative.
narratives.Proposal: Two narrow, independent beads: (1) add a Vitest regression guard that fails if any of the eight deleted alias path literals reappear under src/, locking in the cleanup; (2) update five stale path references in four living docs (TESTING.md, PRD-CLOISTER.md, FIX-ALL-PRD.md, prds/active/pan-509/STATE.md). Explicitly leave historical incident logs and completed PRD archives alone — they're time-locked snapshots, not current reference docs. No live pipeline test is required; PAN-711's own merge cycle exercises the new routes end-to-end.

items.title: Add Vitest guard that fails if deleted alias routes reappear in src/

narrative.Action: Create tests/unit/dashboard/no-alias-routes.test.ts (or extend an existing routes test if a natural home exists). Test walks every .ts file under src/ and asserts that none of the eight deleted alias path literals appear, in either :id or :issueId form. The list is hardcoded inside the test — the test itself documents which paths are forbidden. Forbidden literals: '/api/workspaces/:id/review-status', '/api/workspaces/:issueId/review-status', '/api/workspaces/:id/approve', '/api/workspaces/:issueId/approve', '/api/workspaces/:id/merge', '/api/workspaces/:issueId/merge', '/api/workspaces/:id/review-trigger', '/api/workspaces/:issueId/review-trigger', '/api/workspaces/:id/review-reset', '/api/workspaces/:issueId/review-reset', '/api/workspaces/:id/review-request', '/api/workspaces/:issueId/review-request', '/api/workspaces/:id/request-review', '/api/workspaces/:issueId/request-review', '/api/workspaces/:id/review', '/api/workspaces/:issueId/review'. The test must use full path literal matches (not substring fragments) so it does not false-positive on legitimate routes that happen to share a token like 'review' or 'merge'. Test file itself and any test fixtures are excluded from the scan. Test runs as part of the existing 'npm test' Vitest invocation — no new package, no new script.

subItems.title: Test file exists at tests/unit/dashboard/no-alias-routes.test.ts and runs as part of `npm test`
Test fails (red) if any of the 16 forbidden path literals (8 paths × :id and :issueId variants) is reintroduced anywhere under src/
Test passes (green) on the current branch with no production code changes — confirming the cleanup from PAN-705 commit d18c74cc is in fact complete

Test uses full quoted path literal matching, not substring search; legitimate routes such as `/api/workspaces/:issueId/plan`, `/api/workspaces/:issueId/tldr`, `/api/workspaces/:issueId/refresh-db`, `/api/review/:issueId/status` continue to exist in src/ without triggering the test
Test scans every .ts file under src/ recursively (excluding the test file itself and any node_modules); list of forbidden literals is hardcoded inline so it self-documents which paths are forbidden
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass after the test is added

items.title: Update stale alias-path references in four living docs

narrative.Action: String-replace five lines across four living docs that still cite the renamed paths as if they were current. Pure substitution — do not rewrite surrounding paragraphs, do not 'modernize' adjacent prose, do not touch any other file. Specifically: docs/TESTING.md:132 — `POST /api/workspaces/:id/review-status` becomes `POST /api/review/:id/status`. docs/PRD-CLOISTER.md:178 — `GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/review-status` becomes `GET /api/review/:issueId/status`. docs/PRD-CLOISTER.md:179 — `POST /api/workspaces/:issueId/review-status` becomes `POST /api/review/:issueId/status`. docs/FIX-ALL-PRD.md:124 — `POSTs to /api/workspaces/:id/merge` becomes `POSTs to /api/issues/:id/merge`. docs/prds/active/pan-509/STATE.md:17 — `/api/workspaces/:issueId/review-status` becomes `/api/review/:issueId/status`. EXCLUDED — DO NOT TOUCH: docs/OPERATION-FIX-ALL.md (historical incident log, time-locked), docs/prds/completed/*.md (completed PRD archives, time-locked), docs/research/t3code-drift-plan.md (references different non-renamed routes), and any file mentioning `/api/workspaces/:issueId/plan`, `/api/workspaces/:issueId/tldr`, `/api/workspaces/:issueId/start-agent`, `/api/workspaces/:issueId/refresh-db` — those endpoints still exist at those paths and are not renamed.

subItems.title: docs/OPERATION-FIX-ALL.md is unchanged — the historical incident log entry referencing the old path is preserved as-written, since rewriting it would falsify the time-locked record
No file under docs/prds/completed/ is modified
No source file under src/ is modified by this bead — it is documentation-only
